gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: releaseFractalFunds() public with no access control allows pre-call to inflate totalTitanXBurn before interval allocation snapshot, yielding excess incentive in swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
candidate.claimed_root_cause: releaseFractalFunds() is public with only a time guard; calling it standalone before swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn causes the burnTitanXIntervalUpdate modifier to read an inflated totalTitanXBurn when computing amountAllocated, resulting in a larger incentive transfer to the caller
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker extracts ~154,258 extra TitanX per eligible 110-day window by pre-calling releaseFractalFunds() before swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn

live_preconditions.name: block.timestamp >= lastFractalRelease + FRACTAL_RELEASE_INTERVAL
live_preconditions.observed_value: lastFractalRelease=1780682423; eligible after 1790186423 (~2026-09-01); current block timestamp ~1784022600 — NOT YET MET

live_preconditions.name: currInterval.amountBurned == 0 (current interval not yet executed)
live_preconditions.observed_value: not directly confirmed; lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber=53087 — must query titanXIntervalsBurn(53087) at fork block

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Declared public with no access control modifier; only guard is the time check at L555-556; any caller can invoke it standalone when the interval has elapsed
Modifier calls _intervalUpdateTitanXForBurning() before _ (function body); interval amountAllocated is snapshotted from totalTitanXBurn before swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn body executes
Reads totalTitanXBurn at snapshot time to compute dailyAllocation and amountPerInterval; if totalTitanXBurn was inflated by a prior releaseFractalFunds() call, amountAllocated is larger

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (set by modifier) to compute incentive at L236-237; internal releaseFractalFunds() call at L249-251 fires AFTER amountAllocated is already snapshotted, so a pre-call inflates the snapshot
INCENTIVE_FEE=150 (1.5%), FRACTAL_RELEASE_INTERVAL=110 days, BPS_DENOM=10000 — used to compute incentive delta

attacker_inputs: standalone call to releaseFractalFunds() with no arguments
call to swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn(deadline) in same or subsequent block
call_sequence: 1. attacker.call(contract.releaseFractalFunds()) — moves fractalTitanX (~74B TitanX) into totalTitanXBurn; sets lastFractalRelease=block.timestamp

2. attacker.call(contract.swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn(deadline)) — burnTitanXIntervalUpdate modifier fires _intervalUpdateTitanXForBurning() which reads now-inflated totalTitanXBurn to set amountAllocated; incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000 is larger; internal releaseFractalFunds() check at L249 now fails (timestamp just updated), preventing double-release
state_after: totalTitanXBurn = 464091268443215444654955709007 (inflated by fractalTitanX before modifier snapshot)
attacker receives larger incentive transfer at L254

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX tokens transferred to attacker as incentive at swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn L254
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: ~154,258 extra TitanX per eligible 110-day window (delta between pre-call and no-pre-call incentive, using live totalTitanXBurn and fractalTitanX values with DAILY_ALLOCATION_TITAN_X_BURNING=200)
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol burn pool is depleted by the extra incentive; future burn intervals receive proportionally less TitanX
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: The incentive is computed from amountAllocated which is derived from totalTitanXBurn at snapshot time; the attacker is entitled to the incentive on the pre-fractal-release totalTitanXBurn only; by pre-calling releaseFractalFunds() the attacker causes the snapshot to include fractal funds that the design intends to be released only as a side-effect after the snapshot is taken

setup: vm.createSelectFork('mainnet', PINNED_BLOCK) where PINNED_BLOCK has block.timestamp >= 1790186423 (~2026-09-01)
Ensure lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber interval has amountBurned == 0 (or advance time to create a fresh interval)
Fund attacker with enough ETH for gas; no TitanX needed (incentive is paid from protocol pool)
transaction_sequence: uint256 incentiveBaseline = measureIncentive(false) — call swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn directly, record TitanX balance delta for attacker
uint256 incentiveWithPreCall = measureIncentive(true) — call releaseFractalFunds() then swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn, record TitanX balance delta
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If incentiveWithPreCall == incentiveBaseline, the interval update modifier must be reading totalTitanXBurn after the internal releaseFractalFunds() call rather than before, meaning the ordering assumption is wrong and the finding must be REJECTED. Also reject if _intervalUpdateTitanXForBurning is confirmed to not set amountAllocated from totalTitanXBurn at the time of the modifier call.

rejection_reason: Not rejected. Downgraded from HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE to NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF because: (1) the time precondition block.timestamp >= lastFractalRelease + 110 days is NOT currently met in live state (eligible ~2026-09-01, ~47 days away); (2) _intervalUpdateTitanXForBurning() body was not fully read to confirm it writes amountAllocated into the interval struct from totalTitanXBurn — this is the critical link in the causal chain and must be verified in a fork test; (3) the incentive delta calculation depends on DAILY_ALLOCATION_TITAN_X_BURNING=200 (live) and exact missedIntervals count at fork time, which requires a pinned-block test to confirm.
